cameras.comment: One entry per camera — add as many as you like (USB webcams, OctoPrint/MJPEG streams, snapshot URLs, or watched folders) and mix types freely. Each: id (used in API as ?camera=ID), label, type ('http-snapshot' | 'mjpeg' | 'usb' | 'folder'), and that type's field — url / usbDevice / folderPath. For 'usb', usbDevice is the ffmpeg dshow name (list: ffmpeg -list_devices true -f dshow -i dummy); ffmpegPath is optional (or use PW_FFMPEG).
cameras.label: Anycubic Kobra X

image.comment: Preprocessing tuned for small local vision models. maxSize keeps the long edge small so the model sees the whole bed without huge token cost.

ai.comment: Any Ollama vision model works: gemma3:4b (installed), qwen2.5vl:7b, llava:7b, llama3.2-vision:11b, moondream.

check.comment: Double-check controls. samples = self-consistency votes per frame; frames = cross-frame captures; frameDelayMs = gap between frames; confidenceThreshold = below this, result is flagged 'uncertain'.

confirm.comment: Second-opinion jury. When the primary model suspects a failure, these other local vision models vote to confirm. Add any pulled Ollama vision models, e.g. qwen2.5vl:7b, llava:7b, llama3.2-vision. Empty list = self-check only.

printer.comment: Printer-detection web lookup. When the vision model reads branding off the machine, search that TEXT (never the image) to pin down the exact make/model. Set webLookup:false for fully offline, vision-only detection.

alerts.comment: Send a notification when a print failure (or, optionally, an uncertain verdict) is detected. Do NOT commit real tokens/URLs here — prefer the env vars PW_SLACK_WEBHOOK, PW_DISCORD_WEBHOOK, PW_SLACK_BOT_TOKEN + PW_SLACK_CHANNEL, PW_DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + PW_DISCORD_CHANNEL (and PW_ALERTS_ENABLED=true). Each channel is webhook (incoming-webhook URL) or bot (API token + channel).

mcp.comment: Optional MCP server exposing the cameras/checks/alerts as tools for MCP clients (Claude Desktop/Code). Off by default; enable here or with PW_MCP_ENABLED=true, then run `npm run mcp`. 'target' is the running print-watch HTTP API the tools call (defaults to this instance).

webcam.comment: Optional mjpg-streamer-compatible webcam server so OctoPrint (or anything expecting ?action=snapshot / ?action=stream) can consume a USB cam attached to THIS machine. Snapshot: /webcam?action=snapshot&camera=ID  ·  Stream: /webcam?action=stream&camera=ID. Set enabled:false to turn off.
